query: debug astro dev port still showing 4321 instead of 8808

results.title: Split Astro dev runtime from Minder backend
results.content: Added split local frontend-dev mode where Astro runs on 8808 and Minder backend stays on 8800. Dashboard config now supports dev_server_url redirects for /dashboard routes, admin HTTP app enables CORS for the Astro dev origin, and frontend admin API resolves requests through API_URL bridged into PUBLIC_API_URL by Astro config. Onboarding snippets remain dynamic: in local split mode they derive the backend origin from API requests so snippets point to 8800; in Docker/prod static same-origin mode they continue using request origin. Updated README, local setup, system design, and production deployment docs plus focused tests for config, dashboard scaffold, and console gate.

results.title: Astro dashboard CSS path fix
results.content: The Astro admin console was missing base=/dashboard in src/dashboard/astro.config.mjs, so built CSS and JS assets resolved to root /_astro paths and failed when served by the Python app under /dashboard. Setting base to /dashboard fixes asset URLs; the dashboard bundle or Docker image must be rebuilt after the change because existing dist output still contains old asset paths.

results.title: Fix SMTP wrong version number on Brevo
results.content: Resolved ESOCKET/CONN wrong version number by aligning SMTP TLS mode in oms-api .env: set EMAIL_SMTP_SECURE=false when using port 587 (STARTTLS). Verified with nodemailer transporter.verify() success. Use secure=true only with implicit TLS port 465.

results.title: Astro tsconfig export path corrected
results.content: Astro 5 in the dashboard workspace exports its strict tsconfig as astro/tsconfigs/strict, not astro/tsconfigs/strict/tsconfig.json. The dashboard tsconfig needed to extend astro/tsconfigs/strict to avoid editor and checker errors about missing tsconfig files.

results.title: Docker esbuild ETXTBSY workaround
results.content: The Astro dashboard builder in docker/Dockerfile hit ETXTBSY during npm install when esbuild validated its binary inside the same install step on Node Alpine. Switching to a split install flow fixes it: run npm install --ignore-scripts first, then run node node_modules/esbuild/install.js in a separate layer, then npm run build. Keep this pattern if the dashboard builder remains on Alpine; if the issue persists, switch only the builder stage to a Debian-based Node image.
